query_results: Search results: --- SOURCE 1: Organizations and agencies that work to protect cultural heritage --- URL: https://smarthistory.org/preserve-cultural-heritage/ SUMMARY: Smarthistory – Organizations and agencies that work to protect cultural heritage With 503 contributors from 201 colleges, universities, museums, and research centers, Smarthistory is the most-visited art history resource in the world. “The Foundation of the American Institute for Conservation of Historic and Artistic Works (FAIC) supports conservation education, research, and outreach activities that increase understanding of our global cultural heritage.” “The Association for Research into Crimes against Art (ARCA) is a research and outreach organization which works to promote the study and research of art crime and cultural heritage protection. Smarthistory ARCHES (at risk cultural heritage education series) “The At Risk Cultural Heritage Education Series (ARCHES) is a new Smarthistory learning resource for the study of at-risk cultural heritage, funded by the National Endowment for the Humanities.” FULL CONTENT: Smarthistory – Organizations and agencies that work to protect cultural heritage =============== [![Image 1: Smarthistory Logo](https://smarthistory.org/wp-content/themes/sh-new-theme/assets/images/sh-logo-header.svg)](https://smarthistory.org/)![Image 2: Magnifying Glass icon for search](https://smarthistory.org/wp-content/themes/sh-new-theme/assets/images/mag-glass-left.svg) * About * * ABOUT SMARTHISTORY * [What is Smarthistory?](https://smarthistory.org/about/) * [Is Smarthistory reliable?](https://smarthistory.org/cite-smarthistory/) * [Smarthistory Staff](https://smarthistory.org/staff/) * [Content editors and contributors](https://smarthistory.org/author-list/) * [Board of Trustees and Board of Advisors](https://smarthistory.org/our-board/) * [Museum and institutional partners](https://smarthistory.org/smarthistory-partners/) * [Major donors](https://smarthistory.org/major-donors/) * [Press coverage](https://smarthistory.org/press/) * [Our blog](https://smarthistoryblog.org/) * [Contribute an essay](https://smarthistory.org/contribute-an-essay/) * [Contact us](https://smarthistory.org/contact-us/) * * We believe art has the power to transform lives and to build understanding across cultures. The brilliant histories of art belong to everyone, no matter their background. With 503 contributors from 201 colleges, universities, museums, and research centers, Smarthistory is the most-visited art history resource in the world. * For Learning * * ART HISTORIES * [Start urban sustainability exchange --- URL: https://use.metropolis.org/newsroom/5-approaches-to-preserving-cultural-heritage SUMMARY: Preserving Cultural Heritage | use: urban sustainability exchange | Metropolis Preserving Cultural Heritage Preserving cultural heritage is crucial to achieving the United Nations' Sustainable Development Goals, as it safeguards the unique identity, knowledge, and traditions of communities worldwide. Cultural heritage encompasses tangible elements such as historical monuments, artifacts, and landscapes, as well as intangible aspects like language, customs, and traditional knowledge. Moreover, preserving cultural heritage has far-reaching economic benefits.The sustainable management of cultural assets and well-maintained heritage sites encourages responsible tourism, which can generate income for conservation efforts and support the livelihoods of local communities. Governments play a crucial role in implementing policies and allocating resources to protect and promote cultural heritage. Local communities, too, are vital custodians of their cultural heritage. FULL CONTENT: Preserving Cultural Heritage | use: urban sustainability exchange | Metropolis =============== ### Cookies The **use** platform uses external services to improve your user experience. By agreeing to these services, your data may be accessed by these companies and provided to third parties. For further information please read our Privacy policy. Accept Decline Learn more and customize [](https://use.metropolis.org/) * [Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/company/urban-sustainability-exchange-use) * [X](https://x.com/use_platform) [Search](https://use.metropolis.org/search)[Log in](https://use.metropolis.org/visitors/sign_in) * [Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/company/urban-sustainability-exchange-use) * [X](https://x.com/use_platform) * [Case studies](https://use.metropolis.org/case-studies) * [Community](https://use.metropolis.org/experts) * [Partners](https://use.metropolis.org/partners) * [Newsroom](https://use.metropolis.org/newsroom) * [About](https://use.metropolis.org/about-us) five approaches to Preserving Cultural Heritage ============================ Preserving cultural heritage is crucial to achieving the [United Nations' Sustainable Development Goals](https://sdgs.un.org/goals), as it safeguards the unique identity, knowledge, and traditions of communities worldwide. ![Image 1](https://backend.use.metropolis.org/system/images/4247/original/Vorlage_730x584_photo.jpg) © https://www.syrosisland.gr/ Cultural heritage encompasses tangible elements such as historical monuments, artifacts, and landscapes, as well as intangible aspects like language, customs, and traditional knowledge. By preserving these invaluable resources, we maintain a vital link to our past. This connection to our roots strengthens social cohesion and promotes understanding between generations. Moreover, preserving cultural heritage has far-reaching economic benefits.The sustainable management of cultural assets and well-maintained heritage sites encourages responsible tourism, which can generate income for conservation efforts and support the livelihoods of local communities. However, preserving cultural heritage requires a concerted effort from various stakeholders. **_Governments_** play a crucial role in implementing policies and allocating resources to protect and promote cultural heritage. This includes enacting laws to prevent the destruction or illicit trade of cultural property, providing funding for conservation projects, and supporting educational initiatives that raise awareness about the importance of heritag _e. **Local communities**_, too, are vital custodians of their cultural heritage. By actively engaging in the protection and transmission of traditional knowledge, skills, and practices, they ensure the continuity of their unique cultural expressions. _**International cooperation**_ is equally essential in preserving cultural heritage. [UNESCO](https://www.unesco.org/en), for example, plays a critical role in identifying and protecting [World Heritage Sites](https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/), which are places of outstanding universal value. By working together, nations can share expertise, resources, and best practices to safeguard cultural heritage for future generations. Below are **5 initiatives**that promote promote practical and integrated approaches towards **preserving cultural heritage**being implemented by local governments in partnerships with NGO’s, civil society and the private sector: [Bolivar Park: Reactivating the Historic Centre of Medellin](https://use.metropolis.org/case-studies/bolivar-park-reactivating-the-historic-centre-of-medellin) (Colombia) Improving the quality of life of citizens and the general recovery of public infrastructure ![Image 2](https://backend.use.metropolis.org/system/images/4715/article/parque_bolivar_5.png) **©**City of Medellín © APP Agency [Adopt A Monument](https://use.metropolis.org/case-studies/adopt-a-monument)(Finland) Inclusive cultural heritage for and with the people! ![Image 3](https://backend.use.metropolis.org/system/images/4373/article/Parhaita-k%C3%A4yt%C3%A4nt%C3%B6j%C3%A4.png) © ADOPTOI MONUMENTTI [100 buildings = 100 stories](https://use.metropolis.org/case-studies/100-buildings--100-stories)(Greece) Places, people and digital storytelling ![Image 4](https://backend.use.metropolis.org/system/images/4244/article/100_buildings.png) _© Heritage Management e-Society (HERMeS)_ [A green makeover for heritage buildings](https://use.metropolis.org/case-studies/a-green-makeover-for-heritage-buildings)(Portugal) Energetic refurbishment of heritage buildings is complex and costly to implement. This case study details the quest towards sustainable heritage in Lisbon. ![Image 5](https://backend.use.metropolis.org/system/images/3351/article/Pa%C3%A7os_do_Concelho_cobertura_paineis_solares_LEV5384.png) _© CML Municipality of Lisboa_ [Cultural Resources Climate Change Task Force](https://use.metropolis.org/case-studies/cultural-resources-climate-change-task-force) (USA) The state of California uses cultural resources to drive climate solutions. ![Image 6](https://backend.use.metropolis.org/system/images/5230/article/California_EN_UCLG_2.png) _©_ California State Historic Preservation Office / UCLG Committee on Culture Visit our Cultural Heritage[webpage](https://use.metropolis.org/topics/cultural-heritage)for more information about global cultural heritage initiatives. To access more case studies on heritage you can search the[use platform database](https://use.metropolis.org/search)by SDG 11, or the topic: heritage. FULL CONTENT: ## Top Menu ![](/themes/custom/si_basetheme/logo-sun-clr.svg) ### [Who We Are](/who-we-are) **We cooperate with communities, government agencies, philanthropic organizations, cultural institutions, professional associations and non-profits to save heritage in the most responsive and effective ways.** ### [What We Do](/what-we-do) **We engage in training for heritage stewards and emergency responders; staff stabilization operations for disasters;  conduct research on the causes and mitigation of damage to heritage;  and develop outreach activities to highlight needs and solutions to protect heritage threatened by disaster.** **We aim to elevate the status of cultural heritage issues until they are an integral part of global conversation and action surrounding disaster risk management and emergency response.** **We aim to help ensure that through intervention and action, cultural heritage is spared from destruction and continues to be accessible to the communities it represents.** **We aim to foster the professional development of the global community involved with the protection, preservation, and stewardship of heritage and give them tools to use when a disaster puts their heritage at risk.** **We aim to expand the knowledge and understanding of the causes and responses to cultural heritage destruction, informing strategic solutions and investments in action.** ### [Why It Matters](/why-it-matters) **As our collective memory, cultural heritage represents the spirit and purpose of humanity.** ### [Where We Work](/where-we-work) **Our projects include work in Haiti, Syria, Iraq, Egypt, Mali, Nepal, Beirut, Ukraine, and the United States as well as disaster training for heritage colleagues, emergency responders, and military personnel at home and abroad.** # Smithsonian Cultural Rescue Initiative ![People in yellow vests and hard hats](/sites/default/files/styles/hero_image/public/20231206jn-0572-min.jpg?h=35fd1177&itok=ZWYn1mZh) ## [Heritage Emergency and Response Training (HEART)](https://culturalrescue.si.edu/heritage-emergency-and-response-training-heart) 2025 Call for Applications ![Woman in apron stands in front of stain glass window holding damaged object](/sites/default/files/styles/hero_image/public/20180321_124934-min.jpg?h=48359652&itok=P3FVWMjy) ## [Smithsonian Cultural Rescue Initiative](/who-we-are) The Smithsonian Cultural Rescue Initiative’s mission is to protect cultural heritage threatened or impacted by disasters and to help U.S. and international communities preserve their identities and history. ![US States as puzzle pieces](/sites/default/files/styles/hero_image/public/istock-181394626-min_0.jpg?h=0153f1db&itok=zo6Kh3kY) ## [Heritage Emergency National Task Force](/who-we-are/hentf) The Heritage Emergency National Task Force (HENTF), co-sponsored by the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) and the Smithsonian Institution, is a partnership of more than 60 national service organizations and federal agencies. ![site of burned building in hawaii. mountains visible in the disatance](/sites/default/files/styles/hero_image/public/9a-wo-hing-after-fire-jesse-neizman-min.jpg?h=5f24a0db&itok=SInq_yok) ## [Hawai'i: 2023 Wildfires](/why-it-matters/our-impact/hawaii-2023-wildfires) Supplies and expertise to help stabilize heritage in the aftermath. ![Group of people in front of a concrete building. Sign reads Smithsonian Institution Museum Support Center](/sites/default/files/styles/hero_image/public/mci-symposium-group-001crop-min.jpg?h=e088fd6c&itok=AldgIr1T) ## [Annual Smithsonian Conservation Fellows and Interns Symposium](/what-we-do/response/upcoming-events) ### ### We are pleased to announce that the *[Smithsonian Cultural Rescue Initiative](https://culturalrescue.si.edu/)* (SCRI) is now officially part of the  *[Museum Conservation Institute](https://mci.si.edu/)* (MCI). We look forward to combining our strengths, staff, and resources to preserve cultural heritage.  Together, our interdisciplinary approaches will allow us to develop improved practices for collections care and conservation, advance the scientific research to understand the composition and degradation of objects and mitigate damage, and empower communities to prepare for and respond to cultural heritage threatened or impacted by disasters. The new organization will offer comprehensive collections care expertise to preserve cultural heritage for this generation and those to come. ### Watch this space for updates on our work together. ### [Recognition](/what-we-do/recognition) ![Podium icon](/sites/default/files/styles/square/public/recognition3.png?h=ffba7bc9&itok=9kTjaLJD) **We aim to elevate the status of cultural heritage issues until they are an integral part of global conversation and action surrounding disaster risk management and emergency response.** ### [Response](/what-we-do/response) ![Construction apparel icon](/sites/default/files/styles/square/public/response3.png?h=ffba7bc9&itok=5n3elWYL) **We aim to help ensure that through intervention and action, cultural heritage is spared from destruction and continues to be accessible to the communities it represents.** ### [Resilience](/what-we-do/resilience) ![Checklist icon](/sites/default/files/styles/square/public/resilience3.png?h=ffba7bc9&itok=rR9pFeyE) **We aim to foster the professional development of the global community involved with the protection, preservation, and stewardship of heritage and give them tools to use when a disaster puts their heritage at risk.** ### [Research](/what-we-do/research) ![Magnifying glass icon](/sites/default/files/styles/square/public/research3.png?h=ffba7bc9&itok=6oesh9xO) **We aim to expand the knowledge and understanding of the causes and responses to cultural heritage destruction, informing strategic solutions and investments in action.** ![map with red pin points](/sites/default/files/styles/hero_image/public/eaton-fire-la-ca-rapid-report.jpg?h=557a5c61&itok=8TDPAWXr) ## [Featured Project: Documenting Potential Damage to Cultural Heritage in Los Angeles, CA](/what-we-do/research/scri-research-reports) ## SCRI In the News ### [Saving Hawaii’s History From the Ashes, One Object at a Time(link is external)](https://www.nytimes.com/2025/08/02/arts/design/lahaina-wildfires-hawaii-history-restoration.html) ![palm trees with Hawaiian volcano in the background. ](/sites/default/files/styles/tile/public/lahaina-wildfire-artifacts-lkqf-superjumbo.jpg?h=4e60e8f6&itok=aPYcMXss) ### [Salvaging Damaged Family Items After Flooding(link is external)](https://www.wsaz.com/2025/03/17/salvaging-damaged-family-items-after-flooding/) ![Gloved hand reach in pan filled with water and photo.](/sites/default/files/styles/tile/public/screenshot-2025-03-28-144541_0.png?h=56838a05&itok=_ftt95VN) ### [Reports of ‘large-scale looting’ at Sudan National Museum(link is external)](https://www.museumsassociation.org/museums-journal/news/2024/09/reports-of-large-scale-looting-at-sudan-national-museum/) ![Sudan National Museum ](/sites/default/files/styles/tile/public/sudan_national_museum_8625532907-e1686921836743.jpg?h=7df98ee3&itok=bvJQO4Wt) ## map with cloud cover](https://culturalrescue.si.edu/sites/default/files/styles/hero_image/public/istock-172208211-min.jpg?h=f08c726b&itok=eO0znn4Z) Coordinating and Supporting Organizations ========================================= **We increase protection for cultural heritage by convening collaborations and partnerships.** ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- We cannot do this work alone. Collaboration and partnerships are integral to our four areas of cultural heritage protection. This is a worldwide effort, and the Smithsonian is only one of many organizations that have devoted resources to this important cause. Through strategic partnerships with government agencies and cultural organizations, the Smithsonian: * Participates in the U.S. Department of State Cultural Heritage Coordinating Committee for international crises and chairs the task force for programs and public awareness; * Co-chairs with FEMA the domestic Heritage Emergency National Task Force; * Cooperates and coordinates with UNESCO (U.N. Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization), the Prince Claus Fund through Cultural Emergency Response (CER), ICOM (International Council of Museums) and other international, governmental and professional organizations; * Advises policy makers and government entities about cultural heritage disaster preparedness and response and implementation of the 1954 Hague Convention for the Protection of Cultural Property in the Event of Armed Conflict; * Participates in professional and academic conferences to promulgate awareness of cultural heritage disasters and response; * Cooperates with U.S. and international law enforcement agencies in producing the ICOM Red List and materials to warn of cultural looting and trafficking; * Produces museum exhibitions, publications, and public programs to alert the public about cultural disasters and their consequences. Below is a list of organizations from whom we benefit from close cooperation. * ALIPH – International Alliance for the Protection of Heritage in Conflict Areas * American Institute for Conservation Heritage Tourism | Advisory Council on Historic Preservation --- URL: https://www.achp.gov/heritage_tourism SUMMARY: The National Trust for Historic Preservation defines heritage tourism as “traveling to experience the places, artifacts, and activities that authentically represent the stories and people of the past and present.” A high percentage of domestic and international travelers participate in cultural and/or heritage activities while traveling, and those that do stay longer, spend more, and travel more often. From 2004-2016, the ACHP implemented a national initiative called Preserve America to encourage and support community efforts for the preservation and enjoyment of America’s cultural and natural heritage. From 2006 through 2010, the National Park Service (in partnership with the ACHP) awarded more than $21 million in Preserve America Grants to support sustainable historic resource management strategies, with a focus on heritage tourism. FULL CONTENT: ![ACHP](/themes/achp/logo.svg) # [Advisory Council on Historic Preservation](/) ![Follow us on Facebook](/themes/achp/images/facebook-icon.svg) ![Follow us on Twitter (@usachp)](/themes/achp/images/twitter-icon.svg) ![Subscribe to your YouTube channel](/themes/achp/images/youtube-icon.svg) ![Follow us on Instagram](/themes/achp/images/instagram-icon.svg) ![Follow us on LinkedIn](/themes/achp/images/linkedin-icon.svg) ## Main navigation ### MENU ## Breadcrumb Each year, millions of travelers visit America’s historic places. The National Trust for Historic Preservation defines heritage tourism as “traveling to experience the places, artifacts, and activities that authentically represent the stories and people of the past and present.”  A high percentage of domestic and international travelers participate in cultural and/or heritage activities while traveling, and those that do stay longer, spend more, and travel more often. Heritage tourism creates jobs and business opportunities, helps protect resources, and often improves the quality of life for local residents. From 2004-2016, the ACHP implemented a national initiative called Preserve America to encourage and support community efforts for the preservation and enjoyment of America’s cultural and natural heritage. In partnership with other federal agencies, the initiative encouraged the use of historic assets for economic development and community revitalization, as well as enabling people to experience and appreciate local historic resources through heritage tourism and education programs.  From 2004-2016, over 900 **[Preserve America Communities](https://www.achp.gov/preserve-america/preserve-america-communities)** were designated in all 50 states, the District of Columbia, and two territories, as well as nearly 60 **[Preserve America Stewards](https://www.achp.gov/preserve-america/stewards)**. Many Preserve America Communities are featured in “Discover Our Shared Heritage” National Register **[on-line travel itineraries](https://www.nps.gov/subjects/heritagetravel/preserve-america.htm)**. From 2006 through 2010, the National Park Service (in partnership with the ACHP) awarded more than $21 million in **[Preserve America Grants](https://www.achp.gov/sites/default/files/2018-06/preserve-america-grants-fy2016_0.pdf)** to support sustainable historic resource management strategies, with a focus on heritage tourism.  ###### These links are being provided as a convenience and for informational purposes only; if they are not ACHP links, they do not constitute an endorsement or an approval by the ACHP of any of the products, services or opinions of the corporation or organization or individual. The ACHP bears no responsibility for the accuracy, legality, or content of the external site or for that of subsequent links. Please contact the external site for answers to questions regarding its content, including its privacy policies. ## Related Resources ## Advisory Council On Historic Preservation ## Stay Connected ![Follow us on Facebook](/themes/achp/images/facebook-icon.svg) ![Follow us on Twitter (@usachp)](/themes/achp/images/twitter-icon.svg) ![Subscribe to our YouTube channel](/themes/achp/images/youtube-icon.svg) ![Follow us on Instagram](/themes/achp/images/instagram-icon.svg) ![Follow us on LinkedIn](/themes/achp/images/linkedin-icon.svg) ## Transparency & Reporting ## Federal Government -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --- IMAGES --- IMAGE 1: URL: https://i.ytimg.com/vi/9olH8q_MEiY/maxresdefault.jpg DESCRIPTION: The image shows an ancient brick temple or archaeological site with a focus on cultural heritage conservation, highlighted by a black overlay with white text emphasizing the importance of preserving roots through conservation initiatives. IMAGE 2: URL: https://cdn.slidesharecdn.com/ss_thumbnails/ecoleglobalesculturalheritageconservationefforts-231023090345-7b008d3e-thumbnail.jpg?width=640&height=640&fit=bounds DESCRIPTION: The image features a presentation slide with the title "Ecole Globale's Cultural Heritage Conservation Efforts" alongside a photograph of a residential area with traditional buildings reflected in a water body under a vibrant, partly cloudy sky, emphasizing initiatives to preserve cultural heritage. IMAGE 3: URL: https://d18x2uyjeekruj.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/A4564-Conservation-and-protection-of-heritage-monuments-in-India.-1024x538.jpg DESCRIPTION: The collage features iconic heritage monuments in India, including the Qutub Minar, the Taj Mahal, and intricately carved structures, highlighting the significance of conservation and protection of these historical sites. IMAGE 4: URL: https://image.slidesharecdn.com/ecoleglobalesculturalheritageconservationefforts-231023090345-7b008d3e/75/Ecole-Globale-s-Cultural-Heritage-Conservation-Efforts-pdf-4-2048.jpg DESCRIPTION: A diverse group of young people stands together, symbolizing community involvement and cultural heritage preservation initiatives. IMAGE 5: URL: https://woec.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/image-25.png DESCRIPTION: A group of women dressed in traditional attire participate in a cultural heritage conservation event, holding hands and performing a traditional dance or ceremony. --------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: Join local and global efforts by funding and participating in programs that protect and promote cultural heritage, enhancing community identity and fostering international collaboration.

enriched_points.point_title: Advocacy for Equitable Access to Technology
enriched_points.expanded_content: Champion policies that ensure all communities have access to modern conservation technologies, bridging the digital divide and empowering local heritage preservation efforts.
Foster awareness and participation in cultural heritage initiatives through educational programs, community events, and partnerships, enriching public appreciation and stewardship.
Support research and development of innovative technologies that enhance cultural preservation, such as digital documentation and restoration techniques, ensuring sustainable heritage management.
